items.title: [正面] I've only finished Chapter 1 so far, but I am enjo...
items.summary:
I've only finished Chapter 1 so far, but I am enjoying the gameplay and what I can understand of the story! I feel like the game is pretty solid overall, the music, art, and gameplay are all really good! And the story does seem interesting; however, I feel it greatly suffers from the GPT translation used for the English text. Some words are changed and dialogue can be a bit stilted. (Not to mention the two instances I caught of copy pasted text straight from ChatGPT most likely, "Sure, here's what it says" or the like). 

I feel like the Engish translation would greatly benefit from having some actual human translators take a look at it, to make it flow better and also make it so words aren't constantly changing spelling or something being called by multiple different names. I do want to see the game improve, since I feel like there's potential here! I would like to see the game go on to better and greater heights.

items.summary: realy good (deckbuilding roguelike)-like game i sadly not hear alot about in media, a ton of resourses from the start, active and interesting gameplay loop, a lot of different enemys, and all that in great looking and writen world. i wish this game all the best, as i`ll be playing it for long and definetly recomend for all who like card games to try

items.title: [正面] /!\ Disclaimer: YOU [b]CAN[/b] FULLSCREEN THE GAME...
items.summary:
/!\ Disclaimer: YOU [b]CAN[/b] FULLSCREEN THE GAME AFTER YOU BEAT THE TUTORIAL - Once you get past the tutorial boss go to the Settings > Visuals > FullScreen it /!\

Firstly let's categorize the game: 

This is a mobile gacha game with rogue-like mechanics a la Slay The Spire and a story with tremendous Lovecraftian themes and aesthetics.

Let's get the ugly out of the way first as well. The game is not, for all intents and purposes, released Globally. Usually gacha games are released in their countries first and later ported to specific regions, like Japan/China and eventually the West which is usually named the Global Release. This is not a Global Release. 
The game is, technically, localized but it is still very much the Chinese server and the translation we are getting is used with the aid of AI ChatGPT. 

Why that decision was made, well, it is a rather small game and I imagine the costs of localizing the game properly, as well as Server costs would be too much for the small studio to withstand. Instead they chose to port it to PC and use AI to reach a wider audience.

I imagine once the game grows a stable audience and brings in larger revenue, the localization will be properly made, or they will perfect the current one, as it is mildly wonky. Some phrasing might come off as weird, and the AI will sometimes fumble the sex of characters, but you can 100% pick up on what is being communicated. 
As far as AI translations go I have to say I am impressed, as I have not felt lost, like I wasn't sure what was happening, due to the AI aided translation. That said, there are still some jumbled words in the UI, and the Dev Mail will mostly be in Chinese. 

That being said, the game itself is good. If you can stomach a bit of wonkiness and are a fan of Lovecraftian aesthetics and stories, as well as strategy rogue-like card games, this game will appeal to you. It also seems to be a very generous game, as far as gachas go. 

All in all, the positives outweigh the negatives and the main draw of these games, which are always the characters and story, are fantastic. The artwork is beautiful, and despite the odd trope-y character, for the most part every character in is distinctive, interesting, and brings their own flavor of Lovecraftian madness to the table.

I imagine the game will draw a small audience on Steam, given it is not the type of game for computers, but the gacha fans that can, should give this one a try. Especially if you're into Eldritch Horror. 

Despite the rough translation and odd choice to lock Fullscreen after the tutorial, I recommend this game.

items.title: [正面] Lovecraft waifu game with a really good story. Yes...
